questions.q: The Naga-Pasha was a weapon made of what?
questions.explanation: The Naga-Pasha, the serpent noose, was an arrow-weapon that turned into living snakes binding its victims.

questions.q: Who unleashed the Naga-Pasha against Rama and Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, Ravana's son, fired the serpent-arrows that ensnared the two brothers.

questions.q: Which two brothers were bound by the Naga-Pasha?
questions.explanation: The serpent noose fell upon Rama and Lakshmana, binding them in coils on the battlefield.

questions.q: Who came and freed Rama and Lakshmana from the serpent bonds?
questions.explanation: Garuda, the eagle mount of Vishnu and natural foe of snakes, arrived and dispelled the serpents.

questions.q: Garuda, who freed the brothers, is famous as the enemy of what creatures?
questions.explanation: Garuda is the eternal foe of serpents, so the naga-coils fled at his mere approach.

questions.q: In which great war did the Naga-Pasha episode take place?
questions.explanation: The serpent noose was used during the great war between Rama's army and Ravana's forces at Lanka.

questions.q: Garuda serves as the divine mount of which god?
questions.explanation: Garuda is the vahana (mount) of Vishnu, and Rama is regarded as an avatar of Vishnu.

questions.q: The Naga-Pasha bound the brothers in what form?
questions.explanation: The arrows transformed into living serpents that wound themselves in coils around the brothers.

questions.q: Indrajit, who used the Naga-Pasha, was the son of which Lankan king?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was the son of Ravana, the ten-headed ruler of Lanka.

questions.q: The word 'pasha' in Naga-Pasha most nearly means what?
questions.explanation: 'Pasha' means a noose or snare, and 'naga' means serpent, hence the serpent noose.

questions.q: On which side of the war did Indrajit, wielder of the Naga-Pasha, fight?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was a prince of Lanka and fought on his father Ravana's side against Rama.

questions.q: Indrajit is best known for the rare ability to do what in battle?
questions.explanation: Indrajit could fight while invisible, which let him strike the brothers unseen with the Naga-Pasha.

questions.q: What is the title 'Indrajit' meant to commemorate?
options: His defeat of the god Indra
questions.explanation: The name Indrajit means 'conqueror of Indra,' earned after he defeated the king of the gods.

questions.q: When struck by the Naga-Pasha, Rama and Lakshmana were reduced to what state?
questions.explanation: The serpent arrows pierced and bound the brothers, leaving them fallen and senseless on the field.

questions.q: Whose army watched in grief and confusion as the two brothers lay bound by the serpents?
questions.explanation: Rama's allied vanara army was thrown into despair seeing both leaders fallen and bound.

questions.q: Why did the serpents flee the moment Garuda appeared?
questions.explanation: As the ancient devourer of serpents, Garuda's very presence terrified the nagas into fleeing.

questions.q: After Garuda freed them, what was restored to Rama and Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Garuda's touch restored the brothers' vigor, radiance, and strength.

questions.q: The Naga-Pasha is best classified as which kind of weapon?
questions.explanation: The serpent noose was a divine astra invoked through mantra and loosed as an arrow.

questions.q: Garuda is traditionally described as the son of which sage?
questions.explanation: Garuda is the son of the sage Kashyapa and Vinata, born as the king of birds.

questions.q: Indrajit attacked with the Naga-Pasha chiefly by relying on what tactic?
options: Concealment and surprise from the sky
questions.explanation: Indrajit struck from hiding, unseen in the air, rather than facing the brothers in open challenge.

questions.q: Garuda and the serpents (nagas) share a common father but different mothers; the nagas were born of which mother?
questions.explanation: The serpents were children of Kadru while Garuda was born of Vinata, the root of their enmity.

questions.q: The freeing of the brothers from the Naga-Pasha foreshadows which larger truth about Rama in the epic?
options: That he is mortal and doomed
That divine aid attends him as an avatar of Vishnu
That he will renounce the war
questions.explanation: Garuda, Vishnu's own mount, coming to Rama's rescue underscores Rama's identity as Vishnu incarnate.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, after the brothers fell bound, who first feared they were dead before they revived?
options: The grieving vanara leaders such as Sugriva and Hanuman
questions.explanation: The vanara chiefs, including Sugriva and Hanuman, gathered in grief fearing the brothers had perished.

questions.q: The enmity that makes Garuda dreaded by serpents is rooted in a dispute over what, according to tradition?
options: The bondage of his mother Vinata and the quest for amrita
questions.explanation: Vinata was enslaved to Kadru's serpents, and Garuda's fetching of the amrita to free her cemented the feud.

questions.q: Within the Yuddha Kanda, the Naga-Pasha episode occurs at what stage of the Lanka war?
options: Before the bridge to Lanka is built
Early in the fighting, before Kumbhakarna and Indrajit's later assaults are resolved
questions.explanation: Indrajit's serpent-arrow attack comes in the earlier phase of the Lanka battle, well before Ravana's final fall.

questions.q: Which detail best distinguishes the Naga-Pasha from Indrajit's later, even deadlier illusion-weapon?
options: The Naga-Pasha bound rather than the Brahmastra which struck down a whole army
It was thrown by hand, not shot
It was used by Ravana, not Indrajit
questions.explanation: The Naga-Pasha ensnared the brothers, whereas Indrajit later loosed the Brahmastra that felled the entire vanara host.

questions.q: Garuda is also reckoned in lore as the elder kin of which figure associated with the sun?
questions.explanation: Aruna, the dawn-charioteer of the sun-god Surya, is Garuda's brother, both sons of Vinata.

questions.q: What does Rama say or feel about Garuda after the rescue in the traditional telling?
options: He marvels and asks who this radiant being is
He immediately recognizes him as his own past self
He ignores him and resumes battle silently
questions.explanation: Rama, restored, wonders aloud at the dazzling eagle and inquires about the identity of his deliverer.

questions.q: The Naga-Pasha incident illustrates which recurring feature of Indrajit's warfare in the Ramayana?
options: Use of maya (illusion) and supernatural astras
Refusal to use weapons at all
Fighting only by daylight in fair duels
questions.explanation: Indrajit characteristically wins through illusion and divine astras rather than open strength of arms.

questions.q: After Garuda departs, what advice or effect does his visit leave with Rama's side?
options: A command to retreat from Lanka
Renewed courage and strength to continue the war
A vow never to fight at sea
questions.explanation: Garuda's intervention not only freed the brothers but restored the army's morale and strength to press on.
